- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	autoload -Uz vcs_info
2	
3	zstyle ':vcs_info:*' enable git
4	zstyle ':vcs_info:git:*' formats '%b'
5	zstyle ':vcs_info:git:*' actionformats '%b|%a'
6	
7	setopt PROMPT_SUBST
8	
9	# Parse git status --porcelain -b into counts (single git call)
10	# Usage: _git_counts [git-flags...]
11	# Sets: _staged, _modified, _untracked, _ahead
12	_git_counts() {
13	    _staged=0 _modified=0 _untracked=0 _ahead=0
14	    local line
15	    while IFS= read -r line; do
16	        if [[ "$line" == '##'* ]]; then
17	            [[ "$line" =~ '\[ahead ([0-9]+)' ]] && _ahead=${match[1]}
18	        elif [[ "${line[1,2]}" == '??' ]]; then
19	            (( _untracked++ ))
20	        else
21	            [[ "${line[1]}" == [MADRC] ]] && (( _staged++ ))
22	            [[ "${line[2]}" == [MD] ]] && (( _modified++ ))
23	        fi
24	    done < <("$@" status --porcelain -b 2>/dev/null)
25	}
26	
27	precmd() {
28	    local exit_code=$?
29	    vcs_info
30	
31	    PROMPT='%F{cyan}[%f%F{magenta}%n@%m '

34	    if [[ -d "$HOME/.cfg" ]]; then
35	        _git_counts git --git-dir="$HOME/.cfg/" --work-tree="$HOME" -c status.showUntrackedFiles=no
36	        if (( _modified + _staged + _ahead > 0 )); then
37	            PROMPT+="%F{cyan}.{%f"
38	            (( _modified > 0 )) && PROMPT+="%F{yellow}!${_modified}%f"
39	            (( _staged > 0 )) && PROMPT+="%F{green}+${_staged}%f"
40	            (( _ahead > 0 )) && PROMPT+="%F{magenta}↑${_ahead}%f"

42	        fi

48	    if [[ -n "${vcs_info_msg_0_}" ]]; then
49	        PROMPT+="%F{red}${vcs_info_msg_0_}%f "
50	        _git_counts git
51	        (( _modified > 0 )) && PROMPT+="%F{yellow}!${_modified}%f"
52	        (( _staged > 0 )) && PROMPT+="%F{green}+${_staged}%f"
53	        (( _untracked > 0 )) && PROMPT+="%F{red}?${_untracked}%f"

57	    PROMPT+="%F{cyan}]%f"$'\n'
58	
59	    # 0:    I just dont wanna see if its successful

61	    [[ $exit_code -ne 0 && $exit_code -ne 130 ]] && PROMPT+='%F{red}[%?]%f'

1	# Hyprland → i3wm + Xorg Migration Plan
2	
3	## Current Setup Analysis
4	
5	### Existing Configuration
6	- **Compositor**: Hyprland (Wayland)
7	- **Terminal**: Alacritty
8	- **Launcher**: Wofi
9	- **Notifications**: Dunst (works on both!)
10	- **File Manager**: Caja
11	- **Status Bar**: Waybar
12	- **Screenshot**: grim + slurp + wl-copy
13	- **Autostart**: waybar, dunst, pasystray, owncloud, hyprland-autoname-workspaces, blueman-applet, hyprpaper
14	- **System**: Arch Linux with pacman/yay
15	
16	---
17	
18	## Tool Migration Map
19	
20	| Current (Wayland)                 | →   | X11 Alternative                    | Notes                                  |
21	| --------------------------------- | --- | ---------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
22	| **wofi**                          | →   | **dmenu**                          | Already installed, classic i3 launcher |
23	| **waybar**                        | →   | **i3status**                       | Per your choice, lightweight           |
24	| **grim + slurp**                  | →   | **flameshot**                      | GUI screenshot tool for X11            |
25	| **wl-copy/wl-paste**              | →   | **xclip** OR **xsel**              | Clipboard management                   |
26	| **hyprpaper**                     | →   | **feh** OR **nitrogen**            | Wallpaper setter                       |
27	| **hyprland-autoname-workspaces**  | →   | **i3-workspace-names** (optional)  | Or custom script                       |
28	| **dunst**                         | ✓   | **dunst**                          | Already works on X11!                  |
29	| **alacritty**                     | ✓   | **alacritty**                      | Already works on X11!                  |
30	| **brightnessctl**                 | ✓   | **brightnessctl**                  | Already works on X11!                  |
31	| **playerctl**                     | ✓   | **playerctl**                      | Already works on X11!                  |
32	
33	---
34	
35	## Complete Package List
36	
37	```bash
38	# Core i3 + Xorg
39	yay -S xorg-server xorg-xinit xorg-xrandr arandr
40	yay -S i3-wm i3status i3lock
41	
42	# Compositor (for transparency, blur, animations, shadows)
43	yay -S picom
44	
45	# Tools that need replacing
46	yay -S flameshot              # Replace grim + slurp (GUI screenshot tool)
47	yay -S maim                   # For OCR screenshot script
48	yay -S xclip                  # Replace wl-copy/wl-paste
49	yay -S feh                    # Replace hyprpaper
50	yay -S xdotool                # X11 window manipulation
51	yay -S tesseract              # OCR engine (if not already installed)
52	
53	# Optional but recommended
54	yay -S lxappearance           # GTK theme switcher
55	yay -S qt5ct qt6ct            # Already have these
56	yay -S autorandr              # Monitor profile management (optional)
57	yay -S unclutter              # Hide mouse cursor when idle
58	yay -S xss-lock               # Screen locker integration
59	
60	# Tools that already work (no action needed)
61	# - dunst
62	# - alacritty
63	# - brightnessctl
64	# - playerctl
65	# - pasystray
66	# - blueman-applet
67	# - caja
68	```
69	
70	---
71	
72	## i3 Config Structure Plan
73	
74	**Location**: `~/.config/i3/config`
75	
76	### Key Mapping from Hyprland → i3
77	
78	```
79	# Modifier
80	$mainMod (SUPER) → $mod (Mod4)
81	
82	# Basic Actions
83	SUPER+Return         → $mod+Return              (terminal)
84	SUPER+SHIFT+Q        → $mod+Shift+q             (kill window)
85	SUPER+SHIFT+M        → $mod+Shift+e             (exit i3 - will create menu)
86	SUPER+E              → $mod+e                   (file manager)
87	SUPER+V              → $mod+Shift+space         (toggle floating)
88	SUPER+F              → $mod+f                   (fullscreen)
89	SUPER+R              → $mod+d                   (dmenu launcher)
90	
91	# Focus Movement (vim keys - already use these!)
92	SUPER+h/j/k/l        → $mod+h/j/k/l             (same!)
93	
94	# Window Movement
95	SUPER+SHIFT+H/J/K/L  → $mod+Shift+h/j/k/l       (same!)
96	
97	# Window Resizing
98	SUPER+ALT+h/j/k/l    → $mod+r (resize mode)     (different approach)
99	
100	# Workspaces
101	SUPER+1-9,0          → $mod+1-9,0               (same!)
102	SUPER+SHIFT+1-9,0    → $mod+Shift+1-9,0         (move to workspace)
103	
104	# Monitor Movement
105	SUPER+SHIFT+period   → $mod+Shift+greater       (move workspace right)
106	SUPER+SHIFT+comma    → $mod+Shift+less          (move workspace left)
107	
108	# Screenshots
109	SUPER+SHIFT+S        → $mod+Shift+s             (region screenshot - calls ~/.scripts/x11_screenshot.sh)
110	SUPER+SHIFT+A        → $mod+Shift+a             (OCR extraction - calls ~/.scripts/x11_extracttext.sh)
111	
112	# Special
113	SUPER+CTRL+Q         → $mod+Ctrl+q              (suspend)
114	SUPER+Tab            → $mod+Tab                 (cycle windows - via dmenu)
115	```
116	
117	---
118	
119	## Picom Config Plan
120	
121	**Location**: `~/.config/picom/picom.conf`
122	
123	Based on your Hyprland config (transparency, blur, animations, shadows, 5px rounding, VSync):
124	
125	```ini
126	# Backend & Performance
127	backend = "glx";
128	vsync = true;
129	glx-no-stencil = true;
130	glx-no-rebind-pixmap = true;
131	
132	# Shadows (you had shadows enabled in Hyprland)
133	shadow = true;
134	shadow-radius = 12;
135	shadow-offset-x = -7;
136	shadow-offset-y = -7;
137	shadow-opacity = 0.75;
138	
139	# Fading/Animations
140	fading = true;
141	fade-in-step = 0.03;
142	fade-out-step = 0.03;
143	fade-delta = 4;
144	
145	# Transparency (you used opacity 1.0 in Hyprland, but had blur enabled)
146	inactive-opacity = 1.0;
147	active-opacity = 1.0;
148	frame-opacity = 1.0;
149	
150	# Blur (kawase blur for performance)
151	blur-method = "dual_kawase";
152	blur-strength = 5;
153	blur-background = true;
154	blur-background-frame = false;
155	blur-background-fixed = false;
156	
157	# Rounded corners (5px like Hyprland)
158	corner-radius = 5;
159	rounded-corners-exclude = [
160	  "window_type = 'dock'",
161	  "window_type = 'desktop'"
162	];
163	
164	# Window type settings
165	wintypes:
166	{
167	  tooltip = { fade = true; shadow = true; opacity = 0.95; focus = true; full-shadow = false; };
168	  dock = { shadow = false; clip-shadow-above = true; }
169	  dnd = { shadow = false; }
170	  popup_menu = { opacity = 0.95; }
171	  dropdown_menu = { opacity = 0.95; }
172	};
173	```
174	
175	---
176	
177	## .xinitrc Plan
178	
179	**Location**: `~/.xinitrc`
180	
181	Since you chose startx/xinit, this file will launch your session:
182	
183	```bash
184	#!/bin/sh
185	
186	# Source your profile
187	[ -f ~/.profile ] && . ~/.profile
188	
189	# Merge X resources (if you create ~/.Xresources later)
190	[ -f ~/.Xresources ] && xrdb -merge ~/.Xresources
191	
192	# Set keyboard repeat rate (adjust to preference)
193	xset r rate 300 50
194	
195	# Disable bell
196	xset b off
197	
198	# Monitor setup (adjust for your multi-monitor setup)
199	# Based on your Hyprland: eDP-1 (laptop), DVI-I-1, DVI-I-2
200	# You may need to adjust these with xrandr -q first
201	xrandr --output eDP-1 --mode 1920x1080 --pos 0x0 --rotate normal \
202	       --output DVI-I-1 --mode 2560x1440 --pos 1920x0 --rotate normal --primary \
203	       --output DVI-I-2 --mode 1920x1080 --pos 4480x0 --rotate normal &
204	
205	# Set wallpaper
206	feh --bg-scale ~/Pictures/wallpaper.jpg &
207	
208	# Start compositor (picom)
209	picom &
210	
211	# Start system tray apps (from your Hyprland autostart)
212	pasystray &
213	blueman-applet &
214	dunst &
215	owncloud &
216	
217	# Launch waybar replacement script if you have one
218	# Or i3status will be handled by i3 config itself
219	
220	# Screen locker (optional - locks screen on suspend/sleep)
221	xss-lock -- i3lock -c 000000 &
222	
223	# Hide mouse cursor after inactivity (optional)
224	unclutter --timeout 3 &
225	
226	# Update environment variables for X11
227	export XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP=i3
228	export QT_QPA_PLATFORM=xcb                    # Changed from wayland
229	export QT_QPA_PLATFORMTHEME=qt6ct             # Keep
230	
231	# Finally, exec i3
232	exec i3
233	```
234	
235	**Important**: DO NOT modify `~/.profile` - keep it as-is for Hyprland!
236	
237	The environment variables above (XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP=i3, QT_QPA_PLATFORM=xcb) are set in .xinitrc and only apply when starting i3. When you launch Hyprland via `~/bin/hypr`, your original ~/.profile settings will be used.
238	
239	---
240	
241	## Scripts Strategy
242	
243	**Approach**: Create new X11 versions of scripts with `x11_` prefix to keep Hyprland scripts intact.
244	
245	This allows you to:
246	- Keep Hyprland setup functional if you want to switch back
247	- Use the same script names but prefixed for X11
248	- Update i3 keybindings to call the X11 versions
249	
250	### New Scripts to Create
251	
252	#### ~/.scripts/x11_screenshot.sh
253	New X11 version using flameshot:
254	
255	```bash
256	#!/bin/sh
257	# Simple wrapper for flameshot
258	# Flameshot handles the GUI, region selection, and clipboard automatically
259	flameshot gui
260	```
261	
262	**Note**: Flameshot has built-in features for:
263	- Region selection (interactive GUI)
264	- Automatic clipboard copy
265	- Annotations and editing
266	- Save to file with custom naming
267	
268	If you want to maintain the same auto-save behavior as before:
269	
270	```bash
271	#!/bin/sh
272	path=$HOME"/Pictures/screenshots/"
273	mkdir -p "$path"
274	timestamp=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d-%H:%M:%S")
275	active_window=$(xdotool getactivewindow getwindowclassname 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
276	out="${path}/${timestamp}-${active_window}.png"
277	
278	# Flameshot with auto-save to specific path and clipboard
279	flameshot gui --path "$path" --filename "${timestamp}-${active_window}"
280	```
281	
282	#### ~/.scripts/x11_extracttext.sh
283	New X11 version using maim + tesseract:
284	
285	```bash
286	#!/bin/sh
287	path=$HOME"/Pictures/screenshots"
288	mkdir -p "$path"
289	timestamp=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d-%H:%M:%S")
290	active_window=$(xdotool getactivewindow getwindowclassname 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
291	out="${path}/${timestamp}-${active_window}-ocr.png"
292	
293	# Capture region with maim (waits for selection)
294	maim -s "$out"
295	
296	# Check if screenshot was actually taken (user might have cancelled)
297	if [ ! -f "$out" ]; then
298	    notify-send "OCR Cancelled" "No screenshot taken."
299	    exit 1
300	fi
301	
302	# Copy image to clipboard
303	xclip -selection clipboard -t image/png < "$out"
304	
305	# OCR and copy text
306	extracted_text=$(tesseract "$out" stdout -l eng 2>/dev/null)
307	
308	if [ -n "$extracted_text" ]; then
309	    echo "$extracted_text" | xclip -selection clipboard
310	    notify-send "OCR Complete" "Extracted text is now in your clipboard."
311	else
312	    notify-send "OCR Failed" "No text could be extracted."
313	fi
314	```
315	
316	---
317	
318	## Migration Steps Checklist
319	
320	### Phase 1: Pre-Migration Prep
321	- [ ] 1. Note your current monitor setup for reference
322	  ```bash
323	  hyprctl monitors > ~/monitor-setup.txt
324	  ```
325	
326	### Phase 2: Install Packages
327	- [ ] 2. Install core i3 + Xorg packages
328	  ```bash
329	  yay -S xorg-server xorg-xinit xorg-xrandr arandr i3-wm i3status i3lock
330	  ```
331	
332	- [ ] 3. Install compositor with features you need
333	  ```bash
334	  yay -S picom
335	  ```
336	
337	- [ ] 4. Install replacement tools
338	  ```bash
339	  yay -S flameshot maim xclip feh xdotool tesseract
340	  ```
341	
342	- [ ] 5. Install optional utilities
343	  ```bash
344	  yay -S lxappearance autorandr unclutter xss-lock
345	  ```
346	
347	### Phase 3: Create Configs
348	- [ ] 6. Create ~/.xinitrc (see plan above)
349	
350	- [ ] 7. Create ~/.config/i3/config
351	  ```bash
352	  mkdir -p ~/.config/i3
353	  # (Will provide full config template if you want)
354	  ```
355	
356	- [ ] 8. Create ~/.config/picom/picom.conf (see plan above)
357	  ```bash
358	  mkdir -p ~/.config/picom
359	  ```
360	
361	- [ ] 9. (Optional) Customize dmenu appearance in i3 config or Xresources
362	
363	- [ ] 10. DO NOT modify ~/.profile (keep it as-is for Hyprland)
364	  - Environment variables will be set in .xinitrc for i3 only
365	  - This keeps both Hyprland and i3 working independently
366	
367	- [ ] 11. Create new X11 scripts in ~/.scripts/
368	  - Create x11_screenshot.sh (flameshot version)
369	  - Create x11_extracttext.sh (maim + tesseract version)
370	  - Keep original screenshot.sh and extracttext.sh for Hyprland
371	  - Update i3 config to call x11_* versions
372	
373	### Phase 4: Testing
374	- [ ] 12. Test monitor setup first
375	  ```bash
376	  startx -- :1  # Test on display :1 to keep Hyprland running
377	  ```
378	
379	- [ ] 13. Test keybindings, verify everything works
380	
381	- [ ] 14. Set wallpaper location for feh in .xinitrc
382	
383	- [ ] 15. Test multi-monitor workspace movement
384	
385	### Phase 5: Finalize
386	- [ ] 16. Logout of Hyprland completely
387	
388	- [ ] 17. From TTY, run: `startx`
389	
390	- [ ] 18. Test all autostart apps launched correctly
391	
392	- [ ] 19. (Optional) Add i3 autostart to .zprofile:
393	  ```bash
394	  if [ -z "$DISPLAY" ] && [ "$XDG_VTNR" = 1 ]; then
395	    exec startx
396	  fi
397	  ```
398	
399	- [ ] 20. (Optional) Remove Hyprland packages if satisfied:
400	  ```bash
401	  yay -R hyprland hyprland-autoname-workspaces-git \
402	         xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland
403	  ```
404	
405	---
406	
407	## Additional Considerations
408	
409	### 1. Monitor Configuration
410	Your Hyprland config shows 3 monitors. You'll want to:
411	- Test with `xrandr -q` first to see actual output names (they might differ from Wayland)
412	- Consider using `arandr` (GUI) to set up monitors, then save the script
413	- Or use `autorandr` to save profiles for different setups
414	
415	### 2. i3status Configuration
416	Create `~/.config/i3status/config` for your status bar. Basic example:
417	
418	```
419	general {
420	    colors = true
421	    interval = 5
422	}
423	
424	order += "wireless _first_"
425	order += "ethernet _first_"
426	order += "battery all"
427	order += "disk /"
428	order += "load"
429	order += "memory"
430	order += "tztime local"
431	
432	wireless _first_ {
433	    format_up = "W: %essid %quality"
434	    format_down = "W: down"
435	}
436	
437	ethernet _first_ {
438	    format_up = "E: %ip"
439	    format_down = "E: down"
440	}
441	
442	battery all {
443	    format = "%status %percentage %remaining"
444	}
445	
446	tztime local {
447	    format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"
448	}
449	
450	load {
451	    format = "load: %1min"
452	}
453	
454	memory {
455	    format = "mem: %used"
456	}
457	
458	disk "/" {
459	    format = "disk: %avail"
460	}
461	```
462	
463	### 3. Gaps & Visual Appearance
464	Your Hyprland has 5px gaps. For i3, you'll need `i3-gaps` (which is now merged into mainline i3):
465	
466	```
467	# In i3 config
468	gaps inner 5
469	gaps outer 5
470	```
471	
472	---
473	
474	## What Will NOT Be Modified (Hyprland Safety)
475	
476	To ensure both environments work independently:
477	- ✅ `~/.config/hypr/*` - All Hyprland configs stay untouched
478	- ✅ `~/.profile` - Keep as-is (used by Hyprland's ~/bin/hypr script)
479	- ✅ `~/.scripts/screenshot.sh` - Keep original (new x11_screenshot.sh created instead)
480	- ✅ `~/.scripts/extracttext.sh` - Keep original (new x11_extracttext.sh created instead)
481	- ✅ All Wayland packages (hyprland, wofi, grim, etc.) - Not removed
482	
483	## What Will Be Created (New for i3)
484	
485	- ✅ `~/.xinitrc` - New file for starting i3 with startx
486	- ✅ `~/.config/i3/config` - New i3 config
487	- ✅ `~/.config/picom/picom.conf` - New compositor config
488	- ✅ `~/.config/i3status/config` - New status bar config
489	- ✅ `~/.scripts/x11_screenshot.sh` - New X11 screenshot script
490	- ✅ `~/.scripts/x11_extracttext.sh` - New X11 OCR script
491	- ✅ New X11 packages (i3-wm, picom, flameshot, etc.)
492	
493	**How to switch between them:**
494	- Start Hyprland: Run `~/bin/hypr` from TTY (uses your existing setup)
495	- Start i3: Run `startx` from TTY (uses new .xinitrc)
496	
497	---
498	
499	## Summary
500	
501	You're migrating from a well-configured Hyprland setup. The good news:
502	- ✅ Most of your tools (dunst, alacritty, brightnessctl, playerctl) work on both
503	- ✅ Your keybindings are vim-style and will map cleanly to i3
504	- ✅ Your scripts just need tool replacements (hyprctl→xdotool, grim→maim, wl-copy→xclip)
505	- ✅ You're on Arch, so all packages are readily available
506	
507	---
508	
509	## Next Steps
510	
511	After reviewing this plan, you can:
512	1. Generate a complete i3 config file with all your keybindings migrated
513	2. Create the actual config files (.xinitrc, picom.conf, updated scripts)
514	3. Begin the migration process following the checklist above

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	
3	set -euo pipefail
4	
5	export QT_QPA_PLATFORMTHEME=qt6ct
6	
7	# Ensure a DBus session exists so keyring/secret services can register
8	if command -v dbus-launch >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -z "${DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS:-}" ]; then
9	    eval "$(dbus-launch --sh-syntax --exit-with-session)"
10	    export DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS DBUS_SESSION_BUS_PID
11	fi
12	
13	wait_for_secret_service() {
14	    if ! command -v dbus-send >/dev/null 2>&1; then
15	        return
16	    fi
17	
18	    attempts=0
19	    while [ $attempts -lt 50 ]; do
20	        if dbus-send --session --dest=org.freedesktop.DBus \
21	            --type=method_call --print-reply \
22	            /org/freedesktop/DBus org.freedesktop.DBus.NameHasOwner \
23	            string:org.freedesktop.secrets 2>/dev/null | grep -q "boolean true"; then
24	            break
25	        fi
26	        attempts=$((attempts + 1))
27	        sleep 0.1
28	    done
29	}
30	
31	# Gnome keyring (secrets + ssh agent — capture env so children inherit SSH_AUTH_SOCK)
32	if command -v gnome-keyring-daemon >/dev/null 2>&1; then
33	    eval "$(gnome-keyring-daemon --start --components=pkcs11,secrets,ssh)" || true
34	    export SSH_AUTH_SOCK
35	    wait_for_secret_service
36	fi
37	
38	# Audio (PipeWire)
39	pipewire &
40	pipewire-pulse &
41	wireplumber &
42	
43	# Latency probe (waybar custom/latency module reads /tmp/i3status_latency)
44	~/.scripts/ping-latency.sh &
45	
46	# Pending updates count (waybar custom/updates module reads /tmp/i3status_updates)
47	~/.scripts/update-count.sh &
48	
49	exec start-hyprland
50
